signals.headline: Prediction Market Implications:** This data confirms inflation remains sticky above the Federal Reserve's 2% target and
signals.raw: Prediction Market Implications:** This data confirms inflation remains sticky above the Federal Reserve's 2% target and reduces the probability of near-term rate cuts, which would likely strengthen the U.S. dollar, pressure bond markets, and affect equity valuations—particularly in interest-rate-sensitive sectors.

signals.raw:
March 2026 CPI Surges to 3.3% Year-Over-Year on Historic Gas Price Spike | YES | HIGH confidence**

The Consumer Price Index for March 2026 surged 0.9% month-over-month and 3.3% year-over-year, driven primarily by a **21.2% monthly jump in gasoline prices**—the worst single-month gas increase since 1967.[1][2] Energy prices rose 10.9% overall in March, accounting for nearly three-quarters of the m

signals.headline: Market Impact:** This inflation acceleration has delayed Federal Reserve rate cuts in 2026
signals.raw: Market Impact:** This inflation acceleration has delayed Federal Reserve rate cuts in 2026.
